$doc: Canonical package-owned journal contract. Controls which sections appear in daily journal entries and their order. The root clawd/config/journal-module.json file remains a compatibility mirror during the extraction phase.

vault._comment: Legacy fallback only: shared path inputs win first (JARVOS_JOURNAL_DIR/JARVOS_NOTES_DIR, JOURNAL_DIR/VAULT_NOTES_DIR aliases, JARVOS_VAULT_DIR, or jarvos.config.json paths.journal/paths.notes/paths.vault). These values are used only when no shared path input is configured. The '~/...' tilde form is supported.

required.heading: ## 🎯 Priorities
required.description: User-curated daily priorities. Manual list order is authoritative.

required.heading: ## 📅 Today's Calendar
required.$calendarFilter_doc: Optional list of calendar names to include (e.g. ["Work", "Personal"]). Empty or omitted means all calendars. Requires icalBuddy (macOS only).
required.description: Today's events from Google Calendar via icalBuddy (macOS only)

required.heading: ## 🚩 Flagged
required.description: Medium-confidence captures queued for human review before durable note creation

optional.heading: ## 🔔 Apple Reminders
optional.description: Today's due reminders from Apple Reminders via remindctl

optional.heading: ## 📎 Paperclip Inbox
optional.description: Assigned Paperclip issues needing attention — shows todo/in_progress/blocked issues assigned to Jarvis or Michael. Read-only snapshot; sorted by status (blocked→in_progress→todo) then priority.

filter.doc: Only open/actionable issues. Done/cancelled are excluded. Scope defined in scripts/journal-paperclip-inbox.js.

optional.heading: ## 🗂️ Notes Created
optional.description: Links to vault notes created or updated that day, rendered as a dedicated journal section instead of being mixed into general Notes.

ordering.doc: Sections render in the order listed: all required sections first (in array order), then enabled optional sections (in array order). For manual sections like Priorities, the written list order inside the section is authoritative. To reorder sections, rearrange the arrays above.

structureLock.doc: Structure lock validates that the journal entry matches this config. Required sections must always exist. Optional sections appear only when enabled=true. Unknown sections are flagged (not deleted — content is preserved under the nearest matching section or appended to Notes). Drift is auto-corrected on each heartbeat pass.

current-focus.heading: ## 🎯 Current Focus
current-focus.doc: Legacy Current Focus content is preserved by renaming the section to Priorities on first encounter.

tasks.heading: ## ✅ Tasks
tasks.doc: Legacy Tasks section is removed. Any existing content is migrated to Notes on first encounter.

extensionGuide.doc: To add a new section: 1) Add an entry to sections.optional with a unique id, heading, source, enabled flag, and description. 2) If the source requires a data-fetch script, add the fetch logic to the heartbeat journal-populate step keyed by the section id. 3) Set enabled=true when ready. 4) Structure lock will automatically include it on the next heartbeat pass. No code changes to heartbeat-procedures.md are needed — it reads this config dynamically.
